message:
bd-25f52: Name ownership as lease obligation for Spork registry

Implements lease-backed name ownership on top of the graded obligation
system:

- NameLease: two-phase lease with ObligationToken<LeaseKind> drop bomb
  (release = commit, abort = abort, drop = panic)
- NameRegistry: BTreeMap-based registry with deterministic ordering,
  register/unregister/whereis/cleanup_region/cleanup_task
- NameLeaseError: AlreadyResolved, NameTaken, NotFound
- RegistryEvent: trace-visible events for name lifecycle
- Re-exports from cx module

Prevents stale names: region cannot close until all name leases are
resolved. 15 tests covering lifecycle, collisions, cleanup, re-registration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-257b","title":"Obligation tracker: real-time view of held obligations and potential leaks","description":"# Obligation Tracker

## Overview
Real-time dashboard for tracking all obligations (permits, leases, acks) across the
runtime, with leak detection and aging warnings.

## Design

### Obligation Overview
```rust
pub struct ObligationTracker {
    state: Arc<RuntimeState>,
    console: Console,
}

impl ObligationTracker {
    /// List all active obligations.
    pub fn list_obligations(&self) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.state.all_obligations()
            .map(|o| ObligationInfo {
                id: o.id(),
                type_name: o.type_name(),
                holder_task: o.holder_task(),
                holder_region: o.holder_region(),
                created_at: o.created_at(),
                age: o.age(),
                state: o.state(),
            })
            .collect()
    }
    
    /// Find potentially leaked obligations.
    pub fn find_potential_leaks(&self, age_threshold: Duration) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.list_obligations()
            .into_iter()
            .filter(|o| o.age > age_threshold && o.state.is_active())
            .collect()
    }
    
    /// Get obligations by type.
    pub fn by_type(&self, type_name: &str) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.list_obligations()
            .into_iter()
            .filter(|o| o.type_name == type_name)
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Obligation Types Tracked
- **ConnectionLease**: Database/network connections
- **TransactionLock**: Uncommitted transactions
- **ChannelPermit**: Bounded channel send permits
- **MessageAck**: Unacknowledged queue messages
- **FileLock**: File reservation leases
- **TimerHandle**: Active timer registrations

### Output Format
```
Obligation Tracker
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Active Obligations: 12  │  Potential Leaks: 2  │  Age > 1m: 5 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Type              Count  Oldest    Holder                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ConnectionLease     5    2.3s      Region-2 (HttpHandler)   │
│ TransactionLock     2    45.2s     Task-42 (db_update)      │
│ MessageAck          3    12.1s     Task-17 (process_queue)  │
│ ChannelPermit       2    0.1s      Task-89 (sender)         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ⚠️  POTENTIAL LEAKS (age > 60s):                            │
│   TransactionLock held by Task-42 for 45.2s                 │
│     -> Created at: src/db/queries.rs:89                     │
│     -> Recommendation: Check for missing commit/rollback    │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Interactive Features (TUI)
- Filter by obligation type
- Sort by age, holder, type
- Click to drill down
- Threshold alerts configuration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List all active obligations
- [ ] Group by type
- [ ] Age tracking
- [ ] Leak detection with thresholds
- [ ] Holder information
- [ ] Creation location tracking
- [ ] TUI dashboard
- [ ] Unit tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `tracker::tests::list_all_obligations` - Enumerate obligations
- `tracker::tests::filter_by_type` - Type filtering
- `tracker::tests::detect_potential_leak` - Leak detection
- `tracker::tests::age_threshold_filter` - Age-based alerts
- `tracker::tests::holder_info_display` - Show holder details

### Integration Tests
- `tracker::integration::live_obligations` - Real obligations
- `tracker::integration::concurrent_create_release` - Concurrent ops

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual obligation lookups
- DEBUG: Obligation list queries
- INFO: Potential leak warnings
- WARN: Long-held obligations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:03.582168798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:02:03.822599882Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:02:03.822527798Z","close_reason":"Implemented ObligationTracker with: list_obligations(), find_potential_leaks(), by_type/by_task/by_region filters, summary(), and render_summary(). Includes ObligationInfo, ObligationStateInfo, TypeSummary, and ObligationSummary types. Added 5 unit tests. Exports added to observability module.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-257b","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-257b","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2581","title":"Supervision trees: policies, strategies, restart backoff","description":"# Supervision Trees

## Goal

Implement supervisor actors that monitor children and apply restart policies on failure.

## Background

Supervision is the Erlang/Akka pattern for fault tolerance: parent actors watch children and decide what to do when they fail. This transforms crashes from disasters into routine events.

## Restart Policies

### OneForOne
- Only the failed child is restarted
- Other children unaffected
- Use when children are independent

### OneForAll
- All children restarted when one fails
- Use when children have shared state dependencies

### RestForOne
- Failed child and all children started after it are restarted
- Use when children have ordered dependencies

### Custom
- User-defined Decide trait implementation
- Full control over restart logic

## SupervisionConfig

```rust
pub struct SupervisionConfig {
    /// Restart policy to apply
    pub policy: RestartPolicy,
    
    /// Max restarts in time window before escalating
    pub max_restarts: u32,
    
    /// Time window for counting restarts
    pub restart_window: Duration,
    
    /// Backoff strategy between restarts
    pub backoff: BackoffStrategy,
    
    /// What to do when max_restarts exceeded
    pub escalation: EscalationPolicy,
}
```

## BackoffStrategy

```rust
pub enum BackoffStrategy {
    /// No delay between restarts
    Immediate,
    
    /// Fixed delay between restarts
    Fixed(Duration),
    
    /// Exponential backoff with jitter
    Exponential {
        initial: Duration,
        max: Duration,
        multiplier: f64,
    },
}
```

## EscalationPolicy

What happens when max_restarts exceeded:
1. **Stop**: Stop the failing actor permanently
2. **Escalate**: Propagate failure to parent supervisor
3. **Restart**: Reset restart counter and try again

## Supervisor Actor

```rust
pub struct Supervisor {
    children: HashMap<ActorId, ChildSpec>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    restart_history: VecDeque<Time>,
}

pub struct ChildSpec {
    actor: Box<dyn Actor>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    mailbox_config: MailboxConfig,
}
```

## Failure Detection

Supervisor is notified when:
1. Child returns Outcome::Err from receive()
2. Child returns Outcome::Panicked
3. Child stops unexpectedly (not requested by parent)

## Implementation Notes

- Supervisor is itself an actor (supervisors can supervise supervisors)
- Root supervisor created by runtime
- Restart history uses circular buffer for efficiency
- Backoff implemented via Cx::sleep

## Logging & Observability

Every supervision event traced:
- child_failed(actor_id, reason)
- child_restarting(actor_id, attempt, delay)
- child_restarted(actor_id, attempt)
- max_restarts_exceeded(actor_id, escalation)

## Testing

- OneForOne: verify only failed child restarts
- OneForAll: verify all children restart
- RestForOne: verify correct subset restarts
- Backoff: verify delays between restarts
- Max restarts: verify escalation triggers
- Lab runtime: deterministic supervision behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RestartPolicy enum with 4 policies
- [ ] SupervisionConfig with all options
- [ ] BackoffStrategy with 3 strategies
- [ ] EscalationPolicy with 3 options
- [ ] Supervisor actor implementation
- [ ] Failure detection from child outcomes
- [ ] Restart history tracking
- [ ] Tracing for all supervision events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:44.425130610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:03:29.168789271Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:03:29.168670901Z","close_reason":"Added RestartPolicy (OneForOne/OneForAll/RestForOne), EscalationPolicy (Stop/Escalate/ResetCounter), SupervisionConfig, ChildSpec. 10 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","phase3","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3dcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- xor columns

Prove (in the pragmatic testing sense) that the integration is safe:

- potential V decreases on cancellation-driven workloads (monotone or in expectation, depending on spec)
- no regressions in quiescence / obligation leak oracles

## Acceptance criteria

- At least one lab-runtime test asserts monotone potential decrease on a constructed scenario.
- Existing determinism tests still pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:09.461958463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T10:01:20.969345144Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:01:20.968994202Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","cancellation","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25j2","depends_on_id":"bd-12rg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-25j2","depends_on_id":"bd-2spm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2612k","title":"[SPORK][GenServer] Budget-Driven Timeouts (No Wall Clock)","description":"Integrate `Budget` deadlines into call timeouts and server work budgeting.

- no wall-clock dependence in tests","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:
